# terrible_dellemc_openmanage_ome_firmware (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `baseline_name` (String) Enter the baseline name to update the firmware of all devices or list of devices that are not complaint. This option is mutually exclusive with *dup_file* and *device_group_names*.
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `components` (String) List of components to be updated. If not provided, all components applicable are considered. This option is case sensitive. This is applicable to *device_service_tag*, *device_id*, and *baseline_name*.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `device_group_names` (String) Enter the name of the device group that contains the devices on which firmware needs to be updated. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_id* and *device_service_tag*.
- `device_id` (String) List of ids of the targeted device. Either *device_id* or *device_service_tag* can be used individually or together. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_group_names* and *devices*.
- `device_service_tag` (String) List of service tags of the targeted devices. Either *device_id* or *device_service_tag* can be used individually or together. This option is mutually exclusive with *device_group_names* and *devices*.
- `devices` (String) This option allows to select components on each device for firmware update. This option is mutually exclusive with *dup_file*, *device_group_names*, *device_id*, and *device_service_tag*.
- `dup_file` (String) The path of the Dell Update Package (DUP) file that contains the firmware or drivers required to update the target system device or individual device components. This is mutually exclusive with *baseline_name*, *components*, and *devices*.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `reboot_type` (String) This option provides the choices to reboot the server immediately after the firmware update. This is applicable when *schedule* is `RebootNow`. `GracefulRebootForce` performs a graceful reboot with forced shutdown. `GracefulReboot` performs a graceful reboot without forced shutdown. `PowerCycle` performs a power cycle for a hard reset on the device.
- `schedule` (String) Select the schedule for the firmware update. if `StageForNextReboot` is chosen, the firmware will be staged and updated during the next reboot of the target device. if `RebootNow` will apply the firmware updates immediately.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `error_info` (String) Details of the HTTP Error.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) Overall firmware update status.
- `update_status` (String) The firmware update job and progress details from the OME.
